sections.paragraph:
Σωσθένης, ους, ὁ (Diod S, Diog. L.; Achilles Tat. 5, 17, 5 al.; ins, pap; on the termination-ης s. Mussies 77) Sosthenes

① leader of a synagogue in Corinth at the time of Paul’s first missionary work in that city. He was beaten in the presence of Gallio the proconsul Ac 18:17, but the account (18:12–17) does not say why. From Theodoret to Zahn many scholars, not without good reason, have identified him with the foll.

② a ‘brother’ mentioned in the salutation 1 Cor 1:1, beside Paul himself. See also subscr. He is known to the Corinthians, but need not have been a Corinthian himself, unless he is to be identified w. 1.—LGPN I. M-M.

Diod Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Achilles Tat Achilles Tat , IV a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Mussies Mussies = GM., The Morphology of Koine Greek—List 6

Theodoret Theodoret , of Cyrrhus, IV–V a.d.—List 5

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

foll. foll. = followed, following

subscr. subscr. = subscription

LGPN LGPN = A Lexicon of Greek Personal Names—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 985
